headlines.title: Vegetation fire off Altamont Pass Road near Livermore burns nearly 80 acres
headlines.description: Firefighters were battling a vegetation fire that burned nearly 80 acres off Altamont Pass Road near Livermore on Sunday afternoon, according to Cal Fire. Just before 12:30 p.m., Cal Fire SCU posted on X firefighters were on the scene of a vegetation fire off Altamont Pass Road, northeast of Livermore. The fire was initially reported to be 3 to 5 acres in size. Half an hour later, Cal Fire said the vegetation fire, which was dubbed the Pass Fire, had grown to reach approximately 80 acres in size. At 1:30 p.m., Cal Fire announced on X forward progress was stopped on the fire and it was 30% contained. Authorities added firefighters will remain on the scene working on mop-op and strengthen containment lines.

headlines.title: Person dies after vehicle crashes with tree in Sonoma County
headlines.description: A person died and another was taken to the hospital following a solo-vehicle crash in Sonoma County, according to the California Highway Patrol. CHP said the crash occurred on Highway 101, north of Todd Road, just before 4:20 p.m. Authorities said two people were inside a vehicle that crashed with a tree. CHP said the driver died at the scene and the passenger was taken to a hospital with major injuries.

headlines.title: Thousands prepare for San Francisco's Bay to Breakers amid windy conditions
headlines.description: One of San Francisco’s most iconic events returns this weekend; thousands of people will either run, jog or walk across the city on Sunday for Bay to Breakers. On the eve of Bay to Breakers, participants gathered at the western side of San Francisco to pick up their bibs. For more than a century, the annual race took participants from one end of San Francisco to the other for a total of 7 and a half miles of fun. Sunday’s race will start on The Embarcadero, where some street closures are already in place. However, this year will be a little different: instead of Ocean Beach, the race has a new finish line. “The finish line is now finishing inside Golden Gate Park, so we’re still the Bay to Break from the Bay to Ocean Beach, but we’re going to walk them out about a quarter mile but still finish in Golden Gate Park with the intention that they can play a little longer”. Organizers say it can get a bit windy the ocean, so they expanded inside near the windmill. In the past, some people jumped in the water to mark their achievement or cool off. This time, firefighters encouraged people to stay out of the water. “Some of these 8 to 10 foot waves that are going to be pushing up on the beach, in addition that we have our strong rip currents, and we have those sneaker waves as well. It’s warming up in the city we’re encouraging people to stay out of the water,” said Samuel Menchaca, SFFD PIO.

headlines.title: Areas of the North Bay could face power outages due to wildfire risk, PG&E warns
headlines.description: PG&E warned parts of the North Bay could face power shutoffs from Sunday to Tuesday due to potential wildfire risk. A warning regarding preventive outages is in effect for parts of Napa and Sonoma counties, due to a forecast of strong, dry winds. Shutoffs could occur between Sunday until Tuesday. The utility company specified that it is monitoring weather conditions and that potential service interruptions have not yet been confirmed. PG&E usually provides detailed maps of the affected areas, along with estimated timelines, depending on how the forecast evolves. The utility implements preventive outages during periods of wind gusts, low humidity, and dry vegetation to reduce the likelihood that electrical equipment could spark a wildfire. Sonoma County May 16 Contra Costa County May 15 San Jose May 15 The National Weather Service indicated that these conditions are anticipated for the inland Bay Area and the Central Coast from Friday through Monday. PG&E reminds customers who rely on electricity for medical needs that they may be eligible for additional notifications through its Medical Baseline program.

headlines.title: Fire started by electrical dehumidifier displaces 3 people in Pacifica
headlines.description: An electrical dehumidifier was to blame for a house fire that displaced three people with their dog in Pacifica on Saturday, according to the North County Fire Authority. Officials said the fire was reported at around 5:20 p.m., at a home on Southmoor Drive. The fire was found in the attached garage of the home, and authorities said all residents and one dog had evacuated prior to the arrival of fire personnel. The North County Fire Authority said the cause of the fire was determined to be accidental and due to an electrical dehumidifier, that was continuously plugged in and running at all hours with several combustible materials around it. Authorities said three people were displaced, and nobody was injured. They remind everyone to regularly inspect and properly maintain electrical equipment in accordance with the manufacture’s recommendations.

headlines.title: Warriors' play-in win vs. Clippers was an all-time ‘top 10' game for Steve Kerr
headlines.description: Over the past decade-plus, Steve Kerr has coached some memorable games. From Golden State’s run to its first NBA championship in 40 years during the 2014-15 season to that unforgettable 2022-23 campaign when everyone had counted them out — there’s no shortage of magical moments for Kerr to refer to. And even despite the Warriors’ rocky 2025-26 season with injuries and inconsistencies, Kerr shared that the team’s electric NBA play-in win over the Los Angeles Clippers was a top-10 game for him. “Well, first of all, it’s one of my all-time favorite games,” Kerr said Friday on “The TK Show.” “We’ve had so many amazing games, it’s probably a top-10 game in the 12 years we’ve had, and that’s saying something. It was just the first play-in game, we didn’t even qualify for the playoffs, but that’s how much the game meant to me and to those guys, probably because of the way this season went. “The fact that Steph [Curry] missed 27 straight games, the fact that we were on life support, basically, going into that game, it just was so special. It was such a great win, and ultimately, this is what we do it for, that the players do it to feel that moment. The coaches work every day to achieve that level of play, and it hadn’t worked all year. We just couldn’t reach that level, and then to dig deep and to find it was so meaningful. “It was probably a good metaphor for, like, you can’t get this anywhere else in life — you just can’t. And there’s a reason coaches get addicted to this work, it’s exhilarating. The camaraderie, the companionship, collaboration, it’s beautiful. And that was a wonderful moment.” With their season on the line, the Warriors rallied from a 13-point, fourth-quarter deficit to defeat the Clippers 126-121 at the Intuit Dome. Curry led the comeback with 35 points — 27 in the second half — and Al Horford hit four 3-pointers in the final frame to secure the victory. Draymond Green also displayed a defensive masterclass against Kawhi Leonard. The game was so pivotal that it actually helped sway Kerr’s mind on returning to coach the Warriors for at least the next two seasons. And even though the magic expired two days later with a crushing loss to the Phoenix Suns, that special win in Los Angeles will forever be one of Kerr’s favorites. Download and follow the Dubs Talk Podcast

headlines.title: Swatch closes San Jose store for the day after high demand of new collection
headlines.description: On Saturday, Swatch shut down many of its stores for the rest of the day, including the one in Valley Fair, due to “public safety considerations” and “high demand” of the brand’s new watch collection, which was part of a collaboration with a luxury watch brand. Multiple customers lined up before the store’s opening Saturday morning to get their hands on a watch from the Bioceramic Royal Pop Collection, the brand’s collaboration with Audemars Piguet. However, people there told NBC Bay Area Swatch shut it down when things got too rowdy. Swatch posted on its Instagram page that it had to shut down several stores due to “public safety considerations” following the high demand. A decent line of people was still standing around the store as of 1:30 p.m. San Jose police say they did assist with crowd management at the mall, but says there was no incidents of any violence. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: Vegetation fire west of Lake Sonoma is contained to almost 10 acres
headlines.description: A vegetation fire in the Geyserville area of Sonoma County was fully contained to less than 10 acres on Saturday. Firefighters from Cal Fire’s Sonoma-Lake-Napa Unit (LNU) responded to the fire west of Lake Sonoma at about 7:30 a.m. It took about an hour to get crews in place to attack the fire, which was burning on a ridge in a remote location, according to Cal Fire. The fire was dubbed the Tombs Fire by Cal Fire and contained to about 9.7 acres by about 1:20 p.m. No injuries were reported. A smoldering burn pile that was thought to be extinguished in April was reignited by strong winds, according to Cal Fire. Winds were forecast to be between 20-30 mph in much of Northern California on Saturday amid a red flag warning from the National Weather Service advising of potential fire risk from dry conditions and high-speed winds. Fire crews were expected to remain at the scene for several hours strengthening control lines and doing mop up duty, which involves churning and digging up soil to look for, and monitor, hot spots that could reignite later. Contra Costa County May 15 San Jose May 15 Emergency crews respond to several fires in San Jose

headlines.title: Red flag warning and high winds keep East Bay fire officials on high alert
headlines.description: Contra Costa County Fire officials said they’ll be keeping a close eye on fire conditions a high wind and a Red Flag Warning affecting parts of Northern California. A red flag warning this weekend is impacting parts of the Bay. The warning begins at 5 a.m. Saturday and lasts through Monday night. Fire agencies say the combination of wind, dry conditions and higher temperatures are enough to stay ready. “As a district when it’s wildfire season, we’re going to make sure we’re staffing our wildland apparatus, we’re going to make sure our water tenders are available…and any other equipment we need,” said Captain Victor Daniel with the Contra Costa County Fire Prevention. PG&E is also monitoring parts of Northern California for possible public safety power shutoffs, as utilities watch for winds that could increase fire danger. In July 2024, a brush fire in Bay Point burned dangerously close to homes. The signs of Bay Point Fire are still easy to see two years later. For Kevin Pereyra, who moved in just a few months earlier, it was a frightening introduction to wildfire season in Contra Costa County. “As I was evacuating, I was kind of looking in the backyard and I see the flames on top of the hill and I was like ‘Oh no…it’s coming’,” he said. His neighbor Wayne still has pink stains on his car from the retardant. “It’s really scary because we saw the burn marks and neighbor said it came 60 yards from its backyard,” he said. Residents who have already lived through a close call understand the importance of staying prepared. “Now it’s something we do have to think about everyday. We actually bought stuff for us to take that’s fire-proof,” Pereyra said. “It’s a wakeup call to really think again because we were really scrambling,” Wayne said.

headlines.title: Increased traffic expected as BTS concerts at Stanford Stadium draw big crowds
headlines.description: In the eve of a pop culture sensation returning to the Bay Area, authorities are warning residents about increased traffic in some areas in the Peninsula. On Saturday, K-pop band BTS will play the first of three shows at Stanford Stadium to a total of 150,000 fans throughout the week. BTS fans, who call themselves BTS ARMY, are filling the streets of Palo Alto ahead of the band’s world tour stop for three shows at Stanford Stadium. Around 150,000 fans are expected, and the first concert of the series will begin on Saturday. Because of this, the city warned neighbors about increased traffic around Stanford, on El Camino Real and Embarcadero Road. Businesses nearby are hoping to benefit from all the out-of-town fans. Music & Musicians May 15 Palo Alto May 14 Music & Musicians May 14 “We’re actually running a promotion for the BTS fans, anybody who stops by gets toasted marshmallow fluff on their ice cream, so it’s been super fun to see all their excited faces,” said Jordan Henck, with The Penny Ice Creamery in Palo Alto. However, they also said they are nervous about the traffic; when Coldplay played in the area last year, traffic slowed to a crawl in neighborhoods. In spite of the expected traffic, there is lots to celebrate. The Palo Alto City Hall was lit up in purple for BTS. Hundreds of fans also lined up starting Thursday night into Friday around Stanford just to buy the merch. Lines are expected in the area early Saturday morning, and the early designated parking lots open at 8 a.m., as do the merch booths outside the venue. The concert itself will start at 7 p.m.

headlines.title: Rising gas prices could reach $7 a gallon in California
headlines.description: Rising gas prices could reach $7 a gallon in the coming months. That is the daunting warning from oil industry experts for drivers in the Bay Area and across the state as the pain at the pump continues. The question becomes when do drivers hit a tipping point, meaning when does the price get so high, it alters how we live and what else we buy? Christie Smith takes a closer look at what we can expect with rising gas prices and the state dwindling supply. Watch her report in the video above. Oil & Gas May 14 Business May 6 Economy May 6 Lower-income Americans hit hardest by gas price spike, widening inequalities

headlines.title: Man crashes into grocery store while trying to park in Sunnyvale
headlines.description: On Friday, a man crashed into a Safeway in Sunnyvale while he was trying to park, according to the Sunnyvale Department of Public Safety. The department said the incident happened at the Safeway on S Bernardo Avenue. They said a man drove through the front of the store, kept going about 70 feet in, and stopped at the floral department. Authorities said one customer suffered from a few cuts and bruises, and they were taken to the hospital for further care. Officials added that the driver and the passenger were not injured.
